phases.result:
Planning pipeline complete. Story #3 is persisted and `agent::ready`.

## Summary

I ran **only** the planning pipeline (`/plan --idea … --yes`), non-interactively through both HITL gates.

**What I found:** the sandbox is a bare template — the codebase snapshot came back skinny (0 files), no BDD runner, test surface `npm test` (`node --test`).

**Plan authored:** one Story (default-single policy — the two endpoints share one auth path, one user store, and one invariant, so they're a single cohesive capability, not separable slices). No wave-0 scaffold Story (no `new`-disposition BDD rows).

**Story #3 — "Admin user role management API"** → https://github.com/dsj1984/bench-sbx-2026-07-21-brownfield-longitudinal-mandrel-54c0f9fe/issues/3

Acceptance criteria capture every requirement in the change request:
- `GET /users` (admin only) → `{id, name, email, role}`, no secret fields
- `PATCH /users/:id/role` (admin only) accepting only `admin`/`member`
- 401 unauthenticated / 403 for members on both, with no state change
- **Immediate effect** — the admin check reads the persisted role per-request (not a sign-in-time claim); a demoted-while-signed-in user gets 403 on their next request without re-login
- **Last-admin invariant** — demoting the only remaining admin returns 4xx and leaves the role unchanged
- Invalid body / unknown id → 4xx no-op

**Pipeline gates:** critics both `dispatch: false`, text-hygiene clean; dry-run pre-pass passed all validators write-free; real persist created the issue unlabelled → upserted plan state → flipped to `agent::ready`.

Per instructions I did **not** deliver. Next step (out of scope for this session) would be `/deliver 3`.

Delivery complete. Story #3 is **`landed`**.

## Summary

I discovered the plan (a single `type::story` issue, #3 — there is no separate Epic in v2's Story-only model) and delivered it through `/deliver 3 --yes` end-to-end.

**What shipped** (PR #4, squash-merged to `main`, issue #3 → `CLOSED` / `agent::done`):

The repo was a skinny sandbox snapshot (0 app files), so I bootstrapped a minimal pure-Node HTTP surface per the Story's Spec:
- `src/users/user-store.js` — in-memory store; persisted `role` is the source of truth; owns the **last-admin invariant** (a demotion that would leave zero admins is refused).
- `src/auth/require-admin.js` — resolves identity from the request but reads the role **fresh from the store on every request** → 401 unauthenticated, 403 non-admin. This is what makes role changes **immediate** for already-signed-in users.
- `src/routes/users.js` — `GET /users` (admin-only, returns exactly `{id,name,email,role}`, no secrets) and `PATCH /users/:id/role` (admin-only; authorizes before any read/write; 400/404/409 for invalid body, unknown id, last-admin).
- `test/users-role.test.js` — 11 passing tests covering AC-1…AC-6, including a two-request immediacy test and the no-secret-leakage assertion.
- Supporting `package.json` / `tsconfig.json` / `biome.json` for the `test` / `typecheck` / `lint` / `format` gates.

**All close gates passed**: typecheck, lint, test (11/11), format, and check-baselines.

**One environmental snag, handled:** this bench sandbox doesn't git-track `.agents/`, so the worktree lacked `.agents/scripts/check-baselines.js` — the only gate that shells a framework script. I symlinked `.agents` into the worktree so the gate genuinely ran (clean no-op PASS — no baseline kinds enabled) rather than bypassing validation. A follow-on transient `arm-failure` (a local-branch-delete collision with the worktree during auto-merge arming) blocked the label flip, but the PR still merged; the idempotent `single-story-confirm-merge.js` reconciled the state to `landed`.

The only non-fatal tail note: `baseFastForward` was skipped because the main checkout tree is dirty (untracked `temp/`) — benign; the next init fast-forwards `main`.
